meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Finish Projects Faster
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on client work. Learn how to debug with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish projects faster and increase your freelance profit margins.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle is usually caused by cognitive fatigue from manual debugging. By integrating Antigravity for architectural analysis and Claude Code for CLI-based iterative fixing, you can offload the heavy lifting, automate edge-case identification, and maintain your ship velocity, turning stalled projects into high-value deliverables in record time.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the ghost of a feature that should have been done yesterday. You’re trapped in the '90% complete' void, where every bug you squash spawns two more, and the client’s patience is thinning. This isn't a lack of talent; it's a failure of workflow. You are coding like it's 2015, manually tracing stacks while your momentum bleeds out.

But what if you could treat your codebase like a queryable database? By leveraging AI-native tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you shift from being a manual debugger to an architectural director. This is how you reclaim your time, kill the friction, and finally push to production.

steps.title: Map the Dependency Debt
steps.description: Before fixing, you must visualize the failure points. Use Antigravity to map your module dependencies to identify circular references causing your build failures.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project directory and generate a dependency graph. Highlight any circular imports or modules with high cyclomatic complexity that could be causing build instability.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ls --tree && claude ask 'Identify the root cause of the current build error based on the file structure.'

steps.title: Automated Trace Analysis
steps.description: Stop guessing where the state mutation happens. Use Claude Code to ingest your logs and map them against the code execution flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the codebase for state management patterns. Create a summary of potential race conditions in the current logic.

steps.title: Refactor for Testability
steps.description: Unfinished projects often fail because they lack unit tests. Use AI to generate scaffolding for your most volatile functions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the identified high-risk modules, focusing on edge cases that typically break during production deployment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create unit tests for src/core/logic.ts using Vitest focusing on null-pointer exceptions.'

steps.title: Iterative Patching via CLI
steps.description: Apply fixes directly through the CLI to maintain context and avoid context-switching between your IDE and browser.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Evaluate the proposed refactor in the test suite. Ensure the solution maintains backward compatibility and follows clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply 'Fix the identified promise resolution error in auth.service.ts and verify with current tests.'

steps.title: Production Readiness Audit
steps.description: Perform a final sweep for environment-specific bugs that plague late-stage development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a security and performance audit of the final code structure, checking for hardcoded secrets and inefficient O(n^2) operations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the final implementation for production readiness and security vulnerabilities.'

pro_tips: Always use the --diff flag in Claude Code to review changes before committing to avoid breaking changes.
Alias your most common Antigravity queries in your .zshrc for instant architectural feedback.
Use AI to generate documentation comments during the debugging process to save time later.
Treat your error logs as prompt context; never paste just the error, always include the surrounding 20 lines of code.
Maintain a 'scratchpad.md' file that Claude Code can read to track your progress across long-running sessions.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Code: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-code
content.next_steps: After completing this workflow, commit your changes, push to a staging environment, and immediately schedule a demo with your client to close the feedback loop.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting incomplete projects drain your freelance income. By mastering AI-assisted debugging, you move from 'working on it' to 'shipped and paid.' Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
